lesson.title: Indicative vs Subjunctive Meaning Contrast
lesson.instructions: existence vs non-existence, belief vs doubt

exercises.prompt: I’m looking for a book that explains this topic.
exercises.answer: Busco un libro que explique este tema.

exercises.prompt: I know a person who speaks Japanese.
exercises.answer: Conozco a una persona que habla japonés.

exercises.prompt: There is nobody who can help you.
exercises.answer: No hay nadie que pueda ayudarte.

exercises.prompt: I’m looking for a job that is interesting.
exercises.answer: Busco un trabajo que sea interesante.

exercises.prompt: I believe that he is honest.
I don’t believe that he is honest.
exercises.answer: No creo que él sea honesto.

exercises.prompt: Necesito un médico que hable español.
exercises.answer: I need a doctor who speaks Spanish.

exercises.prompt: There is a restaurant that serves good tacos.
exercises.answer: Hay un restaurante que sirve buenos tacos.

exercises.prompt: I want something that makes me happy.
exercises.answer: Quiero algo que me haga feliz.

exercises.prompt: I know a place that has good food.
exercises.answer: Conozco un lugar que tiene buena comida.
